meta.title: Lancashire LGR Procurement Pipeline Analysis
meta.source: LCC Cabinet March 2026 Procurement Pipeline + Contracts Finder
meta.description: 39 LCC procurement exercises from March 2026 cabinet report, plus Contracts Finder data from all 15 Lancashire councils. Service tier mapping for LGR transition analysis.

current_timeline.note: 18 months from decision to vesting — the most compressed LGR timeline ever attempted for a county of this complexity

recommended_timeline.note: 30 months allows proper contract novation, staff TUPE, IT integration, and service continuity planning

arguments.title: Contract Novation Scale
arguments.detail: An estimated 2,150–4,200 contracts must be novated or relet across 15 councils. Three mega-contracts alone (SEND Schools £920M, Residential Care £700M, SEND Adulthood £660M) extend to 2032–2037. Legal novation requires supplier consent, TUPE consultation, and re-executed schedules.
arguments.risk_if_rushed: Suppliers refuse novation citing material change. Service gaps during legal disputes.
arguments.precedent: Dorset Council required 24 months for ~800 contracts with 9 councils. Lancashire has 2.5–5× the complexity.

arguments.title: TUPE Transfer of 30,000+ Staff
arguments.detail: LCC alone employs ~23,000 staff. Add 12 district councils plus Blackpool and Blackburn: 30,000+ staff require TUPE transfer with 90-day consultation periods, job evaluation, and pay harmonisation.
arguments.risk_if_rushed: Equal pay claims (estimated £15–30M based on Northamptonshire precedent). Staff attrition in critical services.
arguments.precedent: North Yorkshire (2023) found TUPE was the single longest critical path item at 18 months.

arguments.title: IT Systems Integration
arguments.detail: 15 separate HR/payroll, revenues/benefits, planning, housing, social care, and finance systems. Major integrations: Mosaic/LiquidLogic (social care), SAP/Agresso (finance), Northgate (revenues), MARIO (highways). Each requires data migration, testing, and parallel running.
arguments.risk_if_rushed: Parallel systems running for years at double the cost. Data integrity failures.
arguments.precedent: Buckinghamshire (2020) still running parallel finance systems 3 years post-vesting. Estimated £8–12M excess cost.

arguments.title: DfT Highways Settlement Protection
arguments.detail: LCC has a 4-year DfT settlement of £231.8M (2026–2030). If vested April 2028, 2 years transfer mid-settlement creating funding uncertainty. If delayed to April 2029, 3 years are managed by LCC under existing settlement terms — only the final year needs renegotiation.
arguments.risk_if_rushed: DfT may not honour full settlement to successor authorities. Potential £50–90M funding gap.
arguments.precedent: No English county has ever split a multi-year DfT settlement mid-term.

arguments.title: Three Highway Authorities
arguments.detail: Lancashire uniquely has 3 highway authorities: LCC (7,142km), Blackpool (~380km), Blackburn (~680km). 9 highways procurement exercises (£122.3M) must be novated. Geographic lotting across successor areas requires complete contract restructuring, not simple novation.
arguments.risk_if_rushed: Maintenance gaps during contract renegotiation. DfT settlement splitting creates perverse incentives.
arguments.precedent: No precedent for splitting highway authority contracts across this many successor bodies.

arguments.title: SEND Transition Risk
arguments.detail: Three mega-contracts (£2.28B combined) for SEND and residential care cross vesting day. SEND is already in crisis nationally with a £3.4B deficit. Lancashire's 15,000+ EHCPs must transfer seamlessly — any disruption triggers legal liability.
arguments.risk_if_rushed: Judicial review from parents. Inspection failure. Human cost to vulnerable children.
arguments.precedent: Every LGR transition has seen SEND service disruption. Adequate transition time is the only mitigation.

arguments.title: Fire & Rescue Integration
arguments.detail: Lancashire Fire & Rescue Service currently operates county-wide. Under LGR, governance must transfer to new authorities or a combined arrangement must be established. Fire safety cannot have gaps.
arguments.risk_if_rushed: Governance vacuum. Response time degradation during restructure.
arguments.precedent: Dorset and Devon retained combined fire arrangements — took 18 months of negotiation alone.

arguments.title: Democratic Mandate
arguments.detail: Shadow authorities elected May 2027 would have only 11 months before vesting in April 2028 — insufficient time to develop strategic plans, set budgets, or recruit senior leadership. May 2028 elections give 11 months to April 2029.
arguments.risk_if_rushed: New authorities launch without a medium-term financial strategy. Council tax levels set without proper modelling.
arguments.precedent: North Yorkshire shadow council had 15 months (Nov 2021 → Apr 2023). Cornwall had 22 months. Lancashire's 11 months would be historically unprecedented.

arguments.title: Property Estate Division
arguments.detail: LCC owns 1,200 properties valued at ~£2B. Division between successor authorities requires condition surveys, legal title work, environmental assessments, and RICS Red Book valuations. District property portfolios add ~2,000 more assets.
arguments.risk_if_rushed: Assets transferred without proper valuation. Environmental liabilities unidentified. Disputed ownership.
arguments.precedent: Average property division in recent LGR: 12–18 months.

arguments.title: Construction Cost Inflation
arguments.detail: BCIS forecasts civil engineering tender prices rising a further 25% by 2030. Every month of contract uncertainty adds cost. Properly structured long-term contracts with RCTPI indexation clauses protect public value. Rushed novation without proper indexation locks in overpayment.
arguments.risk_if_rushed: Contracts novated without proper cost escalation clauses. Estimated 5–8% premium on rushed procurement.
arguments.precedent: Post-pandemic construction inflation has already added 45% to highway costs. Further delay in settlement compounds the issue.

arguments.title: Quantified Risk Comparison
arguments.detail: Rushed transition (18 months): estimated £48–91M in transition costs, service disruption, litigation, and efficiency losses. Delayed transition (30 months): estimated £34–57M with additional 12 months of running costs offset by lower risk and more orderly savings realisation.
arguments.risk_if_rushed: Net excess cost of rushing: £14–34M vs delayed approach.
arguments.precedent: Based on Dorset (£21.9M/9 councils), Buckinghamshire (£18.6M/5 councils), North Yorkshire (£37.9M/8 councils), Northamptonshire (£42M/8 councils with failures).

arguments.title: Lancashire's Unique Complexity
arguments.detail: 1.5M population, 15 councils (most of any English LGR), 3 existing highway authorities, 2 existing unitaries, a Combined County Authority, 648 councillors, £12.9B tracked spending. This is structurally more complex than any previous English LGR.
arguments.risk_if_rushed: No margin for error in the most complex LGR ever attempted.
arguments.precedent: Previous largest: North Yorkshire (8 councils, 620K pop) took 24 months. Lancashire is 2.4× the population and 1.9× the councils.

delayed_30_months.note: Lower risk premium, no emergency procurement, orderly staff consultation. Additional 12 months of dual running partially offset by early savings from shared services.

upper_tier.description: Services currently provided by LCC (county) plus Blackpool and Blackburn (unitaries). Under LGR, these transfer to new unitary authorities.

exercises.title: SEND Schools Partnership
exercises.note: Largest single contract — crosses vesting day by 4+ years

exercises.title: SEND Transition to Adulthood
exercises.note: Starts on vesting day — will be awarded by outgoing authority

exercises.title: Schools Catering Service
exercises.note: Starts on vesting day, serves schools across all successor areas

exercises.title: Residential Accommodation (Older People)
exercises.note: Starts 6 months after vesting — procurement run during transition chaos

exercises.title: A Roads Resurfacing
exercises.note: Lot structure must align with successor boundaries

exercises.title: Carriageway Patching
exercises.note: Largest highways contract — reactive maintenance essential for safety

exercises.title: Traffic Signals Maintenance
exercises.note: 5,778 signals — safety-critical, cannot have maintenance gap

dft_settlement.note: 4-year DfT highways maintenance settlement. Under delayed vesting (Apr 2029), 3 of 4 years managed by LCC under existing terms. Under current vesting (Apr 2028), only 2 years LCC-managed.

exercises.title: Residual Waste Treatment
exercises.note: Crosses vesting day — district waste collection must integrate with disposal

exercises.title: ICT Managed Services
exercises.note: IT backbone — must serve all successor authorities or be replaced

exercises.title: ERP/Finance System
exercises.note: SAP replacement — will successor authorities share or procure separately?

exercises.title: Revenues & Benefits
exercises.note: Northgate system — council tax billing, housing benefit, business rates

lower_tier.description: Services currently provided by 12 district councils plus Blackpool and Blackburn (unitaries). Under LGR, these merge into successor authorities.

contracts_finder_summary.note: Smallest CF presence — most contracts below threshold

key_district_contracts.note: All 12 districts have separate waste collection contracts. Must integrate with LCC waste disposal.
Many outsourced to trusts (e.g., Burnley Leisure Trust). Long terms complicate novation.
Districts handle council tax collection and housing benefit. Integration with county-level Northgate system critical.
Only some districts retain housing stock. Transfer of HRA complicates financials.
Multiple systems (IDOX, Northgate). Harmonisation needed but not safety-critical.

workstreams.name: Legal Framework
workstreams.note: Statutory instrument, order establishing new authorities

workstreams.name: Shadow Elections
workstreams.note: Electoral registration, boundary mapping, candidate process

workstreams.name: Shadow Authority Setup
workstreams.note: Governance, committees, chief exec recruitment, strategic planning

workstreams.name: Staff TUPE Consultation
workstreams.note: 30,000+ staff, 90-day consultation, job evaluation, pay harmonisation

workstreams.name: IT Integration & Migration
workstreams.note: 15 systems to harmonise, data migration, parallel running, testing

workstreams.name: Contract Novation
workstreams.note: 2,150-4,200 contracts, supplier consent, TUPE within contracts

workstreams.name: Property Division
workstreams.note: 1,200 LCC + ~2,000 district properties, valuations, title, environmental

workstreams.name: Budget Setting
workstreams.note: First budget for new authorities — council tax, reserves, transformation

workstreams.name: Service Redesign
workstreams.note: Align upper/lower tier services into unified delivery models

workstreams.name: Pensions & Equal Pay
workstreams.note: Lancashire Pension Fund restructure, equal pay audit, harmonisation

critical_path.note: IT Integration (W5) and Pensions/Equal Pay (W10) are the longest workstreams at 18 months each. With 6-month setup before they can start, minimum total is 24 months. 18 months is physically impossible without cutting corners.

active_on_vesting_day.note: 28 of 39 LCC exercises will be active on current vesting day (Apr 2028). All require novation or assignment.

awarded_during_transition.note: 8 exercises will be awarded during the transition period by an outgoing authority making decisions that bind successor authorities for years.

start_after_vesting.note: 3 exercises start after vesting day — successor authorities should procure these themselves.
